_readme: Ground truth for every video Curio has actually POSTED — creative metadata + platform-separated analytics, matched to Instagram insights screenshots on 2026-07-12. This file is the durable machine copy; docs/curio/EXPERIMENT_LEDGER.md is the human trail. Import with: npx tsx tools/import_posted_experiments.ts (content-addressed rows; corrections here supersede stale rows via latest-wins). RULES: combined IG+FB view totals are NOT an optimization target; a metrics row is only imported when viewsInstagram is known. Every experiment carries a `provenance` block separating observed / derived / estimated / user-reported values — treat estimated values as approximate.
collectedAtNote: original four-post batch collected 2026-07-12; Dead Water (posted-deadwater) screenshots added 2026-07-16 2026-07-23 batch: Harrison checkpoint 4 + first entries for terminal-lucidity, brine-pool, ant-mill (Business Suite platform-labeled captures).
source: Instagram Reel insights screenshots (samples/Curio Video Stats/* collected 2026-07-12; Dead Water reel screenshots 2026-07-16) + ffprobe/ebur128 measurements of the posted / closest-match local files + reviewer screenshot readings (Codex, 2026-07-12 and 2026-07-16)

experiments.title: Third Man Factor
experiments.postedAtProvenance: inferred — views graph starts Jun 30; exact post time unverified

creative.archetype: atmospheric survival mystery with immediate visual evidence
creative.openingFrame: footprints visible at t=0 — the anomaly before the explanation
creative.hookWording: When survivors are about to give up… someone appears right beside them
creative.visualTimeline: footprints → mountains → sailors → polar environments → distant person → struggling survivor → named phenomenon
creative.reveal: 'The Third Man Factor' named at ~70-80% of runtime; supernatural reading NOT fully eliminated (residual mystery)

analytics.splitNote: IG/FB split from Leon's 2026-07-12 22:49 Reel-insights screenshots: 240 IG + 366 FB = 606. FB supplied 60% of views — consistent with the other three posts. The importer writes the IG-only stream.
analytics.retentionAtEndProvenance: estimated from the retention graph shape — approximate, not a platform number
analytics.reachNote: 165 'Accounts reached' sits on the combined summary card (views card on the same screen shows the 606 total), so the reach surface is AMBIGUOUS — could be IG-only or IG+FB. Kept as reachInstagram per the earlier record, flagged unverified.
analytics.engagementCountsNote: likes/comments/shares/saves/reposts are the counts shown on the IG reel header; whether FB crosspost interactions are included is unverified. Platform-displayed like rate 8.0% does not equal 16/240 (6.7%) or 16/606 (2.6%) — Meta's rate denominator is undocumented; raw counts are the source of truth here.

userReported: skipRate, rates, profileSourceShare, visual timeline and hook wording (Leon's 2026-07-12 analysis of the video + insights)
unverified: reachInstagram (165 shown on combined summary card — surface ambiguous)

experiments.verdict: ONLY clear retention winner (skip 20.5-34.8pp better than every other post; only video with nonzero shares/saves/reposts/comments)

experiments.title: 300 Years Boat (Fata Morgana) v1
experiments.postedAtProvenance: inferred from the export filename date — unverified

creative.openingFrame: dark storm-ship composition, premise not yet stated
creative.hookWording: For 300 years, sailors who saw this thought… (sentence FRAGMENT — premise arrives late)
creative.visualTimeline: single ship composition held through most of the hook; floating ship/fog/false horizon later
creative.reveal: optical illusion (false horizon) — the fascinating idea is delayed

analytics.retentionAtEndProvenance: estimated from the retention graph shape — approximate

derived: like/share/save counts in the import (IG views x rate, rounded)
userReported: skipRate, rates, hook wording, timeline (Leon's analysis)

experiments.verdict: aesthetic liked (6.3% like rate, 2nd best) but comprehension too slow: ~half the audience gone in the first seconds

experiments.title: Can't Forget Anything (hyperthymesia)
experiments.postedAtProvenance: inferred from the export filename date — unverified

creative.archetype: what-if explainer with generic symbolic imagery
creative.openingFrame: glowing brain silhouette — generic stock/AI-health look; no person, conflict, evidence, or mystery
creative.hookWording: What if you could never forget anything? (reads as a superpower; the painful contradiction lands late)
creative.visualTimeline: multiple distinct scenes (full shot list not yet audited); OPENING scene is the verified generic brain silhouette — the retention leak is the opening, not necessarily the whole edit

analytics.retentionAtEndProvenance: estimated from the retention graph shape — approximate

derived: engagement counts in the import (IG views x rate, rounded)
userReported: skipRate, rates, exploreSourceShare, opening-frame description (Leon's analysis)

experiments.verdict: topic has save/share pull (1.6% each + 23.6% Explore sampling) but the generic opening lost the sampled audience — REWRITE AND RETEST the concept, don't discard

experiments.title: 300 Years Boat Retry
experiments.sourceFile: samples/Curio Video Stats/300 Years Boat Retry/My Video-5.MP4

creative.archetype: atmospheric mystery, longer/more-explicit revision (UNCONTROLLED retry: hook, duration, pacing, first shot AND editing all changed at once)
creative.hookWording: more explicit opening, small serif phrases revealed gradually
creative.premiseNote: the basic upside-down-ship premise reads within ~2-3s (reviewer correction); the FAILURE was pacing after the premise — slower, less visually progressive first stretch — not premise opacity
creative.visualTimeline: longer hold on one composition; more atmospheric setup before explanation

analytics.retentionAtEndProvenance: estimated from the retention graph shape — approximate

derived: engagement counts in the import (IG views x rate, rounded)

experiments.verdict: revision made everything WORSE (skip 59.1%→73.4%, likes 6.3%→0.9%, all sharing to zero): pacing after the premise got slower and less visually progressive; also proof that changing five variables at once teaches nothing

experiments.title: Dead Water (In the Arctic)
experiments.postedAtProvenance: inferred from the views graph beginning Jul 16; exact posting time unverified
experiments.sourceFileNote: Closest LOCAL visual match is data/productions/REP-2/REP-2-captions-export-v3-leveled.mp4. v1/v2/v3 (each with a -leveled variant) share the same underlying master, duration (18.833s / 1080x1920@30), and imagery, but their CAPTION treatments (sizes / leveling) are NOT visually identical, so the exact posted export CANNOT be determined without upload evidence. Screenshot runtime (~18s) corroborates the REP-2 family (18.83s) but does not confirm any single file.

creative.archetype: atmospheric strange-science mystery; CONTEXT-FIRST opening (location/atmosphere before the anomaly)
creative.durationProvenance: observed on the local closest-match REP-2 exports (18.833s); screenshot showed ~18s. Posted-file duration unverified.
creative.resolutionProvenance: observed on local REP-2 exports; posted-file unverified
creative.openingFrame: atmospheric Arctic ship + 'IN THE ARCTIC' title card — geographic/atmospheric CONTEXT, not the anomaly
creative.hookWording: 'In the Arctic…' location/context framing; the impossible premise (a ship mysteriously halted — 'dead water') is NOT stated at frame zero
creative.premiseNote: the contradiction (what is impossible) is not presented at frame zero — the opening leads with location + a mostly-static beautiful ship. User verdict: context, not anomaly.
creative.visualTimeline: conservative reconstruction from the UNVERIFIED local REP-2 family (not confirmed as the posted file): 'IN THE ARCTIC' title over an atmospheric ship in ice -> held / slow ship-in-fog compositions -> the ship stalled and held motionless -> captioned explanation of the 'dead water' internal-wave phenomenon near the end. Exact shot list and timing unverified.
creative.visualTimelineProvenance: derived from the local closest-match REP-2 exports — UNVERIFIED as the posted file
creative.reveal: 'dead water' phenomenon (a ship slowed/held by sub-surface internal waves) — factual payoff; whether it carried social currency is unproven

audio.provenance: observed on the LOCAL closest-match REP-2-captions-export-v3-leveled.mp4 via ffmpeg ebur128 — UNVERIFIED as the posted file

analytics.splitNote: IG 224 (10.8%) + FB 1849 (89.2%) = 2073. Facebook supplied ~89.2% of plays. The combined total is NOT a creative win and NOT an optimization target; the IG stream is the creative signal and the importer writes IG-only. IG's 224 ≈ Third Man's 240 → NO Instagram reach breakout.
analytics.reachScopeNote: '162 accounts reached' — platform scope AMBIGUOUS (may be IG-only while 2073 is IG+FB). Do NOT compute views-per-account-reached from these screenshots. Recorded as reachAccounts (surface unverified), NOT as reachInstagram.
analytics.watchRatioProvenance: derived 8s / 18.83s (local family duration) = 0.425; plausible range 0.425-0.444 because Instagram displays only a rounded 0:18

retentionCurve.provenance: estimated from the retention graph shape — approximate, not platform numbers

analytics.engagementCountsNote: Raw counts on the IG reel header: 9 likes; 0 comments/shares/saves/reposts. Displayed rates: like 0.6%, share/save/comment/repost 0.0%. The PLATFORM SCOPE of these engagement counts/rates AND of the average-watch figure is NOT explicitly labeled (IG-only vs IG+FB) — treat the scope as unverified. Meta's rate denominator is undocumented; do NOT recompute the displayed 0.6% from 9/224 (which would give 4.0%). Raw counts are the source of truth. EVERY high-intent action (share/save/comment/repost/follow) is ZERO.
analytics.viewSourcesNote: 87.2% Reels-tab + 9.6% Explore = mostly COLD distribution → the opening-hook failure is a genuine audience verdict on NEW viewers, not an existing-follower artifact.

observed: skipRate (58.5% — directly shown on the supplied screenshot)
duration/resolution/audio of the LOCAL closest-match REP-2 file ONLY (not confirmed as the posted file)
derived: visualTimeline (reconstructed from the unverified local REP-2 family)
estimated: postedAt (inferred from the views-graph start; exact time unverified)
userReported: 'opening is context not anomaly' analysis, component verdicts, capturedAt (Leon/Codex 2026-07-16)
unverified: sourceFile — exact posted export (closest local match: REP-2-captions-export-v3-leveled.mp4)
platform scope of engagement counts/rates and of avgWatchTime — IG-only vs IG+FB not labeled

experiments.verdict: NOT a creative win despite 2,073 combined plays: FB supplied ~89%, IG only 224 (≈ Third Man's 240 → no IG reach breakout), and every high-intent action = 0. Retention pins the failure to the OPENING: ~45% gone by ~3s, then the curve stabilizes (~7-9s onward) → the subject was not the problem, the context-first scroll-stop was. Improved avg viewing DEPTH over the older boat posts but did NOT solve their shared opening problem. REJECTED (causal, repeated evidence): context-first opening, static beauty as scroll-stop, combined-total-as-success. OBSERVED FACT: every high-intent action (share/save/comment/repost/follow) was 0. PROVISIONAL inference (NOT causally proven — the opening failure also shrank the audience that reached the payoff): the payoff may not have produced enough repeatable/social value. NOT REJECTED: the Dead Water topic. vs Third Man (240 IG / 38.6% skip / ~59.5% watch / real shares+saves+comments) → Dead Water (224 IG / 58.5% skip / ~42.5% watch / zero high-intent).

experiments.title: Harrison Okene (The Hand Grabbed Back)
experiments.postedAtProvenance: CONFIRMED — Business Suite shows 'Published on: Fri Jul 17, 7:54pm' (captured 2026-07-19). Also confirmed: post was created on Instagram and crossposted to Facebook ('Published by Instagram'). FB content_id 122119453365356339, Page 'TryCurio'.
experiments.postedCaptionText: A rescue diver entered a sunken tugboat expecting to recover bodies. Then, from the darkness, a hand reached out and grabbed him. Harrison Okene had survived nearly three days underwater, 100 feet below the surface, breathing from a tiny pocket of trapped air. Everyone thought he was dead. He was still waiting to be found. #booktok #psychology #mystery #iosapp #booktok
experiments.liveStatus: LIVE, plateaued ~77.9K FB views as of 2026-07-23 — FROZEN: do not edit/delete/rebuild/repost/boost
experiments.sourceFileNote: Posted frame zero = the glitchy (Veo) hand grab — Leon-confirmed 2026-07-19 — which matches the v3 lineage (local closest match: data/productions/HARRISON-OKENE/preview-okene-v3.mp4, 270x480@30, 19.633s). Screenshot shows captions NOT present in that local file, so the posted file is a captioned derivative that does NOT exist on this machine (Downloads/iCloud/samples swept 2026-07-19). All local captioned exports are v5-based and RULED OUT by frame-zero mismatch, including ~/Downloads/a_hand_inside_the_wreck…_20260717_1717.mp4 (1080x1920@30, 19.633s, v5 imagery). Posted resolution, captions, and audio loudness are therefore UNVERIFIED; if captioned via the Captions.ai app without a re-mux, audio was likely renormalized to ~-13.3 LUFS.

creative.archetype: true survival mystery; ANOMALY-FIRST opening — an impossible visible human action on frame zero
creative.durationProvenance: observed on the local v3 lineage (19.633s, shared by every Okene export); IG displays a rounded 0:19. Posted-file duration itself unverified.
creative.resolution: 270x480@30 on the local v3 closest match — POSTED-FILE RESOLUTION UNVERIFIED
creative.openingFrame: a hand emerges from the dark wreck and grabs the diver back — physically impossible-looking human contact at t=0; visible AI defects in the generated hand (Veo lineage)
creative.hookWording: posted caption text UNVERIFIED; locked VO opens 'A hand inside the wreck grabbed the diver back'
creative.visualTimeline: hand-grab anomaly -> alive underwater -> 60 hours -> 100 feet down -> breathing trapped air -> engineered silence -> surfacing could kill him -> pressurized bell -> two more days decompressing (reconstructed from the v3-lineage production; posted export unverified)
creative.reveal: the rescue itself could have killed him — decompression as the second trap after the apparent rescue

audio.provenance: locked audio master QA values — POSTED-FILE audio unverified (Captions.ai app renormalizes to ~-13.3 LUFS if no re-mux was done)

creative.producedWith: factory v3 lineage (Veo hero motion) + unknown external captioning pass

analytics.splitNote: Checkpoint 4 (2026-07-23, Business Suite platform-labeled): IG 183 (0.2%) + FB 77,742 (99.8%) = 77,925. Second FB recommendation surge ~Jul 19 (9,570->29,619->74,892 across two timeline buckets) then plateau. Importer writes the IG-only stream; FB stream fed via /api/performance/ingest.
analytics.reachScopeNote: 113 = Instagram reach, CONFIRMED platform-labeled on the Business Suite Instagram tab (2026-07-19). Earlier ambiguity resolved.
analytics.avgWatchScopeNote: 13s average watch CONFIRMED Instagram-native (Business Suite IG tab, 2026-07-19; IG total watch 27m52s). The 36.5% skip figure remains from the IG-oriented screenshot surface. IG metrics still MUST NOT explain Facebook distribution — Facebook now has its own native block below.

retentionCurve.provenance: estimated from the IG retention graph shape (2026-07-18 screenshot) — approximate, Instagram-scoped

analytics.retentionAtEndProvenance: estimated from graph shape — approximate, Instagram-scoped
analytics.engagementCountsNote: IG-NATIVE (Business Suite IG tab, 2026-07-19): interactions 1 — a single like; comments/shares/saves/reposts/follows all 0 on Instagram. SCOPE CORRECTIONS: the earlier 'likes 20' header reading was NOT IG-only (superseded); the 153-reactions figure from checkpoint 2 was FB-dominant (FB-native reactions now 185). Facebook advocacy is NOT zero: 4 shares + 8 saves (see facebookNative) — ~0.03% of FB views, so the advocacy gate is still WEAK relative to distribution, but 'all zero' is superseded.
analytics.viewSourcesNote: Instagram-scoped top-sources card (2026-07-18): 85.5% Reels tab + 8.1% Explore = cold discovery test on IG; IG sampled it and stopped expanding.

checkpoints.note: checkpoint 1 — PRESERVED as time-series evidence, never overwrite
checkpoint 2 (morning, user-reported dashboards) — FB grew ~6.5x in ~1 day while IG added +2. The zeros here were dashboard displays; superseded in detail by checkpoint 3's platform-labeled capture.
checkpoint 3 — FB-NATIVE capture via Meta Business Suite (platform-labeled tabs). FB +9,634 views in ~1.5h since checkpoint 2; still accelerating.

checkpoint 4 — plateaued. RETENTION-CURVE CAVEAT: today's insights page showed the 'Views over 15 seconds' CONDITIONED toggle (flat ~1.0 through 13s) — that is NOT the unconditioned curve; checkpoint 3's unconditioned series (100% through 3s -> 56% completion) remains the retention ground truth. Audience: men 85%, 45-64 heavy, US 22.7K/UK 4.6K/CA 2.5K. Watch-time distribution: Recommendations 99.5% / Followers 0.5%.

facebookNative.source: Meta Business Suite post insights, Facebook tab (platform-labeled), Leon's logged-in Chrome session — user-directed capture; Graph API token still expired
facebookNative.retentionNotes: Unconditioned platform series, 1s bins 0-20s. 100% through 3s; largest step 3s->4s (-11.6pp); tiny kink at the 10s engineered-silence beat; 56.1% completion. The 'Views over 15 seconds' toggle view stays >=92%. Platform summary text: 'Most of your audience dropped off at the 0:02 mark and your average watch time is 0:15.' Screenshots: samples/Curio Video Stats/Harrison Okene/

audienceBasedOn3sViews.note: heavily 45-64 (men 45-54: 22.6%, men 55-64: 26.3%) — an OLDER male audience; IG's audience skews 18-34

facebookNative.pageBenchmark: typical recent-post views P25=12 / P75~266 -> this post ~135x P75

viewsTimeSeries.note: Business Suite chart series (lags the 35,799 headline). Slow build for ~24h (~5k), then sharp acceleration Jul 18 evening -> Jul 19.

instagramNative.source: Meta Business Suite post insights, Instagram tab (platform-labeled)

observed: FULL FACEBOOK-NATIVE BLOCK (Business Suite FB tab, platform-labeled, 2026-07-19 11:30): views 35,799 / viewers 17,645 / avg watch 15s / retention curve incl. 56.1% completion / shares 4 / saves 8 / reactions 185 / follows 0 / rec-vs-follower watch split 99.3-0.7 / audience / geo / time-series
FULL INSTAGRAM-NATIVE BLOCK (Business Suite IG tab): views 179 / reach 113 / 1 like / 0 all other actions / avg watch 13s / 27m52s watch time
postedAt Fri Jul 17 7:54pm + 'Published by Instagram' crosspost origin + posted caption text + 0:20 displayed runtime (Business Suite)
duration/resolution/audio of LOCAL v3-lineage files only (posted file itself not obtained)
META token expiry (Graph API error re-tested 2026-07-19)
estimated: the OLD IG retention curve from the 2026-07-18 screenshot (graph shape) — kept for history; FB has real platform numbers now
userReported: posted Reel starts with the glitchy hand (Leon 2026-07-19) — basis for the v3-lineage identification
unverified: sourceFile — exact posted captioned export (v3-lineage confirmed by frame zero, file NOT on this machine)
posted file resolution / audio loudness (caption TEXT now known from Business Suite)
the 36.5% skip figure's exact surface (2026-07-18 screenshot)

experiments.verdict: FACEBOOK DISTRIBUTION *AND RETENTION* WINNER; ADVOCACY STILL WEAK. Three gates on FB-NATIVE data (2026-07-19): [1] Scroll-stop PASS — retention holds 100% through 3s and the recommender keeps widening distribution (35,799 views, ~135x the Page's typical P75, 99.3% from Recommendations). [2] Retention PASS — avg watch 15s (76% of runtime), 56% completion, main drop 3-9s, only a tiny kink at the 10s silence beat; the AI-hand defects did NOT measurably break FB retention. [3] Advocacy WEAK — no longer literally zero (FB: 4 shares + 8 saves + 185 reactions) but ~0.03% of FB views and 0 follows; IG advocacy literally 1 like. IG plateaued at 179 (reach 113) while FB found a large 45+ male US/UK audience — the platforms sampled DIFFERENT demographics; never explain one with the other's metrics. Frame-zero impossible human evidence + escalating single-story danger is confirmed as Curio's best opening architecture; the remaining fix is the payoff (transferable fact) and clean 1080x1920 assets — see REP-4 brief.

experiments.title: Terminal Lucidity (For a moment, they were themselves again)
experiments.postedAtProvenance: CONFIRMED — Business Suite 'Published on: Thu Jul 16, 7:41pm'; crossposted (TryCurio + trycurio.app). FB content_id 122119178811356339.
experiments.postedCaptionText: For a moment, they were themselves again. Families have reported brief, unexpected returns of recognition or communication in people with advanced dementia. Researchers call the broader phenomenon paradoxical lucidity; near the end of life, it is often called terminal lucidity. Scientists still don't know exactly how it happens. Comforting—or unsettling? #TerminalLucidity #Neuroscience #DementiaResearch #HumanMind
experiments.sourceFileNote: Terminal Lucidity v6.2 lineage produced 2026-07-16 (see PRODUCTION_DOCTRINE narrative-engine rules); exact posted export not verified this capture.

creative.archetype: named human phenomenon; quiet emotional mystery; NOT anomaly-first — opens on a bedridden elderly person (context/emotion-first)
creative.durationProvenance: ESTIMATED from the v6.x lineage (~17s target); posted-file duration unverified
creative.openingFrame: elderly person in bed, dim room — emotionally legible but no visible contradiction at t=0
creative.hookWording: For a moment, they were themselves again
creative.visualTimeline: bedridden elder -> family recognition moment -> the phenomenon named (paradoxical/terminal lucidity) -> unresolved cause (reconstructed; posted export unverified)
creative.reveal: researchers call it paradoxical lucidity / terminal lucidity — cause unknown

audio.provenance: lineage QA target — posted-file audio unverified
audio.style: quiet bed, engineered pauses (v6.2 loop-breath build)

creative.producedWith: factory narrative engine v6.2 lineage + external captioning pass

analytics.splitNote: Business Suite platform-labeled: FB 325 / IG 141. BOTH surfaces flatlined within ~6h (IG stuck 135-138 from hour ~5; FB crawled to 325 over 6 days with no recommendation surge). The only post neither engine picked up.
analytics.avgWatchNote: IG-native watch metrics not captured this checkpoint; combined watch-time table shows Recommendations 99.1% / Followers 0.9%.
analytics.engagementNote: DERIVED IG split: interactions FB 4 / IG 2 (platform-labeled); combined likes 6, comments/shares/saves 0 -> FB 4 likes, IG 2 likes. Link clicks 0.
analytics.provenance: Meta Business Suite object insights (Total performance + Facebook tab), browsed live by the producer in Leon's Chrome session 2026-07-23. IG/FB interaction splits marked DERIVED are arithmetic: platform-labeled totals minus the FB-tab natives; each derivation stated inline.

experiments.verdict: NO DISTRIBUTION ON EITHER SURFACE. Emotion-first opening with no frame-zero contradiction; 466 combined views, 6 likes, zero advocacy, zero follows. Consistent with (not proof of) the anomaly-first rule: this is the only recent post without a visible anomaly at t=0 and the only one FB's recommender never surged.

experiments.title: Brine Pool (The ocean has lakes—and some are deadly)
experiments.postedAtProvenance: CONFIRMED — Business Suite 'Published on: Sun Jul 19, 6:31pm'; crossposted. FB content_id 122119903587356339.
experiments.postedCaptionText: The ocean has lakes—and some are deadly. They're called brine pools: ultra-salty pockets of water so dense they barely mix with the ocean above. Most animals that enter can suffocate, while entire communities survive along their edges. The deep sea keeps rewriting what "impossible" looks like. Send this to someone who would swim over one anyway. #BrinePools #OceanScience #DeepSea #MarineBiology
experiments.sourceFileNote: BRINE-POOL v5 lineage (17.333s master per doctrine 57 notes) BUT the Business Suite player shows 0:15 — posted-export identity unverified; reconcile before reusing lineage numbers.

creative.archetype: strange-science; anomaly-first IMAGE (a lake under the ocean) with a one-line complete-thought opening card
creative.durationProvenance: OBSERVED — Business Suite preview player 0:15 (conflicts with the 17.333s v5 local master; unresolved)
creative.openingFrame: top-down dark circular pool on the seafloor — 'LAKES BENEATH / THE OCEAN' one-line complete-thought card (rule 51/55.4 build)
creative.hookWording: The ocean has lakes—and some are deadly
creative.visualTimeline: dark crater pool -> density boundary side-view -> animals at the edge -> suffocation claim (hedged) -> edge communities -> closing card (v3/v5 lineage; posted export unverified)
creative.reveal: brine pools named mid-piece; residual mystery in the closing line

audio.provenance: v5 QA (−16.0 LUFS / −1.6 dBFS) — posted-file audio unverified
audio.style: dark bed, engineered silence, loop breath (rules 56/57 build)

creative.cta: caption share-bait line ('Send this to someone who would swim over one anyway')
creative.producedWith: factory BRINE-POOL v5 lineage + Captions.ai app pass (rule 55 procedure)

analytics.splitNote: THE FIRST REAL INSTAGRAM DISTRIBUTION EVER: IG 1,608 (previous IG best: Third Man 240) — steady organic growth over ~2.5 days (18->1,573), NOT a spike. FB 2,014 with a small late bump (~483->2,014 after day 1). 96.2% of watch time from Recommendations.
analytics.avgWatchNote: IG-native watch metrics not captured this checkpoint (combined-row avg play 8s is cross-surface; 3-second views 779/3,622 = 21.5% — the weakest scroll-stop proxy of the recent posts).
analytics.engagementNote: DERIVED IG split: interactions FB 9 / IG 23 (platform-labeled); combined 28 likes + 4 saves; FB feed shows 9 reactions and FB-tab natives were not drilled -> IG = 19 likes + 4 saves (=23 ✓), FB = 9 likes + 0 saves. FOLLOWS 3 (total-performance card) — the ONLY follows on any Curio post ever; per-surface split UNVERIFIED. Link clicks 0.
analytics.provenance: Meta Business Suite object insights (Total performance + Facebook tab), browsed live by the producer in Leon's Chrome session 2026-07-23. IG/FB interaction splits marked DERIVED are arithmetic: platform-labeled totals minus the FB-tab natives; each derivation stated inline.

experiments.verdict: INSTAGRAM'S FIRST YES + THE ONLY CONVERSION SIGNAL. Modest absolute numbers, but: 6.7x the previous IG record with sustained (not sampled-and-dropped) IG delivery, IG-led engagement (23 of 32 interactions), and the account's only 3 follows. Weak FB scroll-stop (21.5% 3-sec rate). The share-bait caption line produced 0 shares.

experiments.title: Ant Mill (Every ant followed perfectly. That was the problem.)
experiments.postedAtProvenance: CONFIRMED — Business Suite 'Published on: Mon Jul 20, 6:52pm'; crossposted. FB content_id 122120143335356339.
experiments.postedCaptionText: Every ant followed perfectly. That was the problem. 🐜 Separated army ants can loop onto their own scent trail, reinforcing a circle with no leader and no exit. It's called an ant mill—and it can continue until ants collapse from exhaustion. If you saw one forming, would you break the circle? Send this to someone who loves nature's strangest true stories. #Nature #Ants #curio
experiments.sourceFileNote: ANT-MILL production lineage exists locally (referenced by MICROGRAVITY tooling notes); exact posted export not verified this capture.

creative.archetype: nature anomaly; ANOMALY-FIRST — the spinning ring of ants is fully visible and legible at t=0. NOT survival-of-a-human: first non-survival breakout.
creative.durationProvenance: ESTIMATED from the FB retention-curve span (~16-17s); posted-file duration unverified
creative.openingFrame: dense circular ant mill filling the frame, motion carrying the fact
creative.hookWording: Every ant followed perfectly. That was the problem.
creative.visualTimeline: spinning mill -> scent-trail mechanism -> no leader, no exit -> named (ant mill) -> exhaustion stake (reconstructed; posted export unverified)
creative.reveal: 'ant mill' named, followed by the exhaustion consequence
creative.cta: caption question + explicit share-bait line (ONE-OUTCOME deviation, logged deliberately for the A/B note)
creative.producedWith: local production lineage + external captioning pass (unverified)

analytics.splitNote: Business Suite platform-labeled: FB 74,737 (99.8%) / IG 147 (flatlined ~143 within hours). FB surge began ~27h post-publish (~600 -> 72,708 in roughly an 8-bucket run), then plateau. Watch time 99.8% Recommendations.
analytics.avgWatchNote: IG-native watch metrics not captured (147 views = noise tier). FB-native: avg watch 12s, total 6d 13h, 3-second views 33,829 (45.2% of views), retention page showed the CONDITIONED 'views over 15 seconds' toggle (~1.0 through 15s) — unconditioned curve NOT captured; do not quote it as retention.
analytics.engagementNote: DERIVED IG split: interactions FB 135 / IG 2; FB-tab natives 110 likes + 6 comments + 4 shares + 15 saves (=135 ✓); combined 111/7/4/15 -> IG = 1 like + 1 comment. Link clicks 0. Follows 0.
analytics.provenance: Meta Business Suite object insights (Total performance + Facebook tab), browsed live by the producer in Leon's Chrome session 2026-07-23. IG/FB interaction splits marked DERIVED are arithmetic: platform-labeled totals minus the FB-tab natives; each derivation stated inline.

experiments.verdict: SECOND FACEBOOK BREAKOUT — AND THE FIRST NON-SURVIVAL ONE. Same shape as Harrison (delayed recommendation surge, older-male US cold audience, 0 follows / 0 link clicks), which weakens 'survival subject' and strengthens 'immediate visible anomaly' as the operative variable. Advocacy still weak at scale: 4 shares / 15 saves / 6 comments on 74.7K FB views WITH explicit share-bait in the caption (vs Harrison's 5 shares with none) — bait moved nothing.
